message:
feat(widgets): add focus management system + alloc budget fix

- Focus graph (graph.rs): HashMap adjacency, O(1) navigate, Floyd
  cycle detection, tab ordering, build_tab_chain (bd-1n5t.1)
- Focus manager (manager.rs): focus/blur, history stack, group-
  confined navigation, nested trap support (bd-1n5t.2, bd-1n5t.3)
- Spatial navigation (spatial.rs): quadrant-based search with
  weighted distance scoring, explicit edge precedence (bd-1n5t.4)
- Fix alloc_budget CUSUM threshold: 5.0 → 8.0 for correct ARL₀
  under two-sided test (bd-14la)
- Fix notification_queue tuple destructuring

98 focus tests + 19 alloc_budget tests pass. Clippy clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2a5","title":"API documentation and examples suite","description":"PHASE 7 - POLISH: DOCUMENTATION

## Background
Comprehensive documentation is essential for library adoption. Must include API docs, guides, and runnable examples covering all major use cases.

## Technical Requirements

### API Documentation
- All public types documented with /// comments
- Module-level documentation with usage examples
- Cross-linking between related types
- Platform-specific behavior documented
- Feature flag documentation
- Safety documentation for any unsafe code

### Examples Suite
- examples/hello.rs - Minimal 'Hello World'
- examples/counter.rs - Basic state management
- examples/input.rs - Text input handling
- examples/layout.rs - Flex/Grid layouts
- examples/styling.rs - Themes and styles
- examples/table.rs - Data tables
- examples/async.rs - Async runtime integration
- examples/inline.rs - Inline mode demo

### Guide Documentation
- Getting Started guide
- Architecture overview
- Migration guide from Ratatui
- Performance tuning guide
- Testing guide

## Design Rationale
Documentation is a feature. Examples serve as both learning resources and regression tests. Keeping docs close to code via rustdoc ensures accuracy.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 100% public API documented
- [ ] All examples compile and run
- [ ] Examples cover each major widget
- [ ] Guide for each major topic
- [ ] docs.rs badge in README
- [ ] Changelog maintained

 {"id":"bd-2ao","title":"Implement Kitty keyboard protocol support (optional)","description":"# Implement Kitty Keyboard Protocol Support (Optional)

## Background
The plan mentions Kitty keyboard protocol in TerminalCapabilities detection and ADR-004. This is an optional enhancement that provides richer key event information.

## What Kitty Keyboard Protocol Provides
- Distinguish key press vs repeat vs release
- Modifier key events (Shift pressed, Shift released)
- Proper handling of key combinations
- Unicode codepoint of key
- Alternate key representations

## Why It's Optional (P3)
- Only supported by Kitty, WezTerm, Ghostty, foot
- Traditional CSI sequences cover most use cases
- Additional complexity for marginal benefit
- Feature-gated to avoid bloating core

## Technical Design

### Detection
```rust
impl TerminalCapabilities {
    pub fn detect() -> Self {
        // ...
        let kitty_keyboard = term.contains("kitty") ||
            std::env::var("KITTY_WINDOW_ID").is_ok() ||
            // Could also detect WezTerm, Ghostty
            false;
    }
}
```

### Protocol Activation
```rust
impl TerminalSession {
    pub fn enable_kitty_keyboard(&mut self) -> io::Result<()> {
        // CSI > flags u
        // flags: 0b1111 = disambiguate + report event types + report alternate keys + report all keys
        self.writer.write_all(b"\x1b[>15u")?;
        self.kitty_keyboard_active = true;
        Ok(())
    }
    
    pub fn disable_kitty_keyboard(&mut self) -> io::Result<()> {
        // CSI < u - pop keyboard mode
        self.writer.write_all(b"\x1b[<u")?;
        self.kitty_keyboard_active = false;
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### Enhanced Event Type
```rust
pub struct KeyEvent {
    pub code: KeyCode,
    pub modifiers: Modifiers,
    pub kind: KeyEventKind,  // Press, Repeat, Release
    // Kitty-only extensions:
    pub base_codepoint: Option<char>,
    pub shifted_key: Option<char>,
}

pub enum KeyEventKind {
    Press,   // Key pressed
    Repeat,  // Key held down (auto-repeat)
    Release, // Key released
}
```

### Parser Extension
```rust
impl InputParser {
    fn parse_kitty_key(&mut self, seq: &[u8]) -> Option<Event> {
        // CSI unicode-codepoint ; modifiers ; event-type u
        // Parse the enhanced sequence
        // ...
    }
}
```

## Graceful Degradation
- If Kitty protocol not available, fall back to standard CSI
- KeyEventKind defaults to Press
- Optional fields are None for legacy input

## Tests Required
- [ ] Detection works for Kitty terminal
- [ ] Protocol activation/deactivation
- [ ] Parse enhanced key sequences
- [ ] Modifier key press/release events
- [ ] Graceful fallback when not supported
- [ ] RAII cleanup on exit

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Feature-gated behind `kitty-keyboard` feature
- [ ] Detection in TerminalCapabilities
- [ ] Activation/deactivation in TerminalSession
- [ ] Enhanced KeyEvent fields
- [ ] Parser handles Kitty sequences
- [ ] Falls back gracefully
- [ ] Documented in compatibility matrix

 {"id":"bd-2b82","title":"Text Effects: Demo Showcase Integration","description":"## Background
The visual_effects screen needs to showcase all new text effects in an impressive, navigable demo that serves as both documentation and a selling point for FrankenTUI.

## Implementation

### Navigation Structure
```
Tab 1: Gradients    - Horizontal, Vertical, Diagonal, Radial, Animated
Tab 2: Animations   - Wave, Bounce, Shake, Cascade, Pulse, Breathing
Tab 3: Typography   - Shadow, Glow, Outline, Mirror, ASCII Art
Tab 4: Special FX   - Glitch, Chromatic, Scanline, Particle, Matrix
Tab 5: Presets      - All 10 presets with descriptions
Tab 6: Combinations - User can toggle effects on/off
```

### Key Features
1. Spacebar cycles through effects within current tab
2. TransitionOverlay shows effect name + description on switch
3. Number keys 1-6 switch tabs
4. 'e' key toggles easing comparison mode
5. 'c' key enters combination builder

### Visual Layout
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ [Gradients] [Animations] [Typography] ...    │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                             │
│         EFFECT DEMO TEXT                    │
│         (with current effect)               │
│                                             │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Effect: Rainbow Gradient                    │
│ Speed: 0.3  │  Easing: EaseInOut           │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Space: next │ ←→: adjust │ Tab: category   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### ASCII Art Header
Use AsciiArtText with Block style for 'EFFECTS' title, animated gradient.

## Files
- crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/visual_effects.rs

## Testing

### Unit Tests
- test_tab_navigation: Keys switch tabs correctly
- test_effect_cycling: Spacebar advances effect index
- test_transition_triggers: Effect change starts transition

### E2E Tests (in scripts/demo_showcase_e2e.sh)
```bash
# Navigate through all tabs and effects
echo 'Testing visual effects screen navigation...'
# Send key sequence: 1,space,space,2,space,3...
# Verify no panics, all effects render
```

## Acceptance
- All 6 tabs implemented
- All effects showcased
- Smooth transitions between effects
- No rendering glitches
- Help text updated with new keybindings

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Demo Showcase Integration
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

-{"id":"bd-2bzl","title":"Text Effects: AsciiArtText Integration","description":"## Background
AsciiArtText exists but doesn't fully integrate with the effect system. Multi-line text needs:
- Consistent effect application across all rows
- Reflection support (already partly addressed)
- Gradient mapping that makes sense for 2D text

## Implementation

### StyledText from AsciiArt:
```rust
impl StyledText {
    /// Create StyledText from AsciiArtText for multi-line rendering
    pub fn from_ascii_art(art: AsciiArtText) -> StyledMultiLine {
        StyledMultiLine {
            lines: art.render_lines(),
            effects: Vec::new(),
            // ...
        }
    }
}

/// Multi-line text with effects
pub struct StyledMultiLine {
    lines: Vec<String>,
    effects: Vec<TextEffect>,
    easing: Easing,
    time: f64,
    reflection: Option<Reflection>,
}
```

### Effect Application for 2D:
When applying effects to multi-line text:
- Gradients use 2D coordinates (x=char, y=row)
- Wave can affect y-offset per row
- Glow/Shadow renders around entire block

### Render Method:
```rust
impl Widget for StyledMultiLine {
    fn render(&self, area: Rect, frame: &mut Frame) {
        for (row_idx, line) in self.lines.iter().enumerate() {
            let row_y = area.y + row_idx as u16;
            // Apply effects with 2D context
            self.render_line(line, area.x, row_y, row_idx, self.lines.len(), frame);
        }
        
        // Render reflection if present
        if let Some(ref reflection) = self.reflection {
            self.render_reflection(area, frame, reflection);
        }
    }
}
```

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests
- test_ascii_art_to_styled: Conversion preserves lines
- test_multiline_gradient_2d: Gradient uses x and y
- test_multiline_effects_apply: Each line gets effects
- test_multiline_with_reflection: Reflection renders below

## Visual Tests
- Snapshot 5-line 'HELLO' with vertical gradient
- Snapshot same with reflection

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: AsciiArtText Integration
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-2d2v","title":"Cross-Terminal Clipboard Abstraction","description":"# Cross-Terminal Clipboard Abstraction

## Background & Motivation

Clipboard access varies across terminals and platforms:
- OSC 52 (modern terminals)
- pbcopy/pbpaste (macOS)
- xclip/xsel (X11)
- wl-copy/wl-paste (Wayland)

**The solution:**
- Unified Clipboard API
- Auto-detect best method
- Graceful fallback chain

**Relationship to existing beads:**
- Requires bd-10i.4.4 (TerminalCapabilities detection)
- Extends bd-2rs (OSC 52 implementation)

## Technical Design

```rust
pub struct Clipboard {
    backend: ClipboardBackend,
}

pub enum ClipboardBackend {
    Osc52,
    MacOS,  // pbcopy/pbpaste
    X11,    // xclip
    Wayland, // wl-copy
    Unavailable,
}

pub enum ClipboardError {
    NotAvailable,
    WriteError(String),
    ReadError(String),
}

impl Clipboard {
    pub fn detect(caps: &TerminalCapabilities) -> Self {
        // Try OSC 52 first if terminal supports it
        // Fall back to platform-specific tools
    }
    
    pub fn set(&self, text: &str) -> Result<(), ClipboardError> { ... }
    pub fn get(&self) -> Result<String, ClipboardError> { ... }
}
```

## Logging Requirements

```rust
// Backend detected (INFO level)
tracing::info!(backend = ?backend, "Clipboard backend detected");

// Clipboard write (DEBUG level)
tracing::debug!(backend = ?backend, bytes = text.len(), "Clipboard write");

// Fallback triggered (WARN level)
tracing::warn!(
    primary = ?primary_backend,
    fallback = ?fallback_backend,
    reason = reason,
    "Clipboard fallback triggered"
);
```

## E2E Tests

```bash
# Test clipboard roundtrip
./ftui-harness --test-mode clipboard --text "Hello, World!"
# Assert: get() returns same text

# Test with large content
./ftui-harness --test-mode clipboard --size 100KB
# Assert: content preserved

# Test fallback chain
FTUI_CLIPBOARD_BACKEND=none ./ftui-harness --test-mode clipboard --expect-fallback
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] OSC 52 implementation works in modern terminals
- [ ] Platform fallback (macOS, Linux X11, Linux Wayland, Windows)
- [ ] Auto-detection of best method
- [ ] Clear error messages for unsupported environments
- [ ] Works in SSH sessions (OSC 52 preferred)
- [ ] Binary content base64 encoded
- [ ] All tests pass","notes":"## Important: Timeout Handling for OSC 52

OSC 52 clipboard read requires careful timeout handling:

```rust
impl Clipboard {
    /// Read from clipboard with timeout
    /// OSC 52 responses may not arrive or be slow
    pub fn get_with_timeout(&self, timeout: Duration) -> Result<String, ClipboardError> {
        match self.backend {
            ClipboardBackend::Osc52 => {
                // Send OSC 52 query
                self.send_osc52_query()?;
                
                // Wait for response with timeout
                match self.wait_for_response(timeout) {
                    Ok(data) => Ok(base64::decode(&data)?),
                    Err(TimeoutError) => {
                        // Fall back to platform clipboard
                        self.fallback_get()
                    }
                }
            }
            _ => self.platform_get(),
        }
    }
}
```

Default timeout: 100ms (configurable)

Also add Timeout variant to error enum:
```rust
pub enum ClipboardError {
    NotAvailable,
    WriteError(String),
    ReadError(String),
    Timeout,  // OSC 52 response didn't arrive
}
```

This should be added to the implementation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:12:18.141848615Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T18:03:57.293768568Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T18:03:57.293733673Z","close_reason":"Implemented cross-terminal Clipboard abstraction with backend detection, OSC52/external fallback, env override, logging hooks, and get/set/clear APIs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2d2v","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:40:42.820849187Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2d2v","depends_on_id":"bd-2rs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:13:20.059018812Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2d66","title":"fix: use saturating arithmetic in virtualized render_end calculation","description":"In virtualized.rs line 840-841, the render_end calculation uses unchecked addition: (scroll_offset + items_per_viewport + overscan).min(total_items). While overflow is unlikely in practice, using saturating_add would be more defensive and consistent with the rest of the codebase.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:21:29.707761812Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T18:22:11.130653494Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T18:22:11.130634058Z","close_reason":"Already fixed: render_end uses saturating_add; verified in virtualized.rs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2dc","title":"Implement Status Line and Panel widgets","description":"PHASE 5 - STATUS LINE + PANEL WIDGETS

## Background
Status lines and panels are essential for agent harness UIs. Status line shows current mode/status. Panel provides structured chrome around content areas.

## Technical Requirements

### Status Line Widget

pub struct StatusLine<'a> {
    left: Vec<StatusItem<'a>>,
    center: Vec<StatusItem<'a>>,
    right: Vec<StatusItem<'a>>,
    style: Style,
    separator: &'a str,
}

pub enum StatusItem<'a> {
    Text(Text<'a>),
    Spinner(SpinnerState),
    Progress { current: u64, total: u64 },
    KeyHint { key: &'a str, action: &'a str },
    Spacer,
}

impl StatusLine<'_> {
    pub fn left(mut self, item: StatusItem) -> Self;
    pub fn center(mut self, item: StatusItem) -> Self;
    pub fn right(mut self, item: StatusItem) -> Self;
}

// Typical usage
StatusLine::new()
    .left(StatusItem::Text("[INSERT]".into()))
    .center(StatusItem::Text("file.rs".into()))
    .right(StatusItem::KeyHint { key: "^C", action: "Quit" })
    .right(StatusItem::Text("Ln 42, Col 10".into()))

### Panel Widget

pub struct Panel<'a> {
    title: Option<Title<'a>>,
    content: Box<dyn Widget + 'a>,
    footer: Option<Text<'a>>,
    border_style: BorderStyle,
    focus_style: Option<Style>,
    focused: bool,
}

impl Panel<'_> {
    pub fn new(content: impl Widget) -> Self;
    pub fn title(mut self, title: impl Into<Title>) -> Self;
    pub fn footer(mut self, footer: impl Into<Text>) -> Self;
    pub fn focused(mut self, focused: bool) -> Self;
}

### Layout Integration
Panels work with Flex/Grid:

Flex::vertical()
    .add(Panel::new(log_viewer).title("Logs").flex(1.0))
    .add(Panel::new(input).title("Command").height(3))
    .add(StatusLine::new()...)

### Focus Management
- focused property changes border style
- Tab navigation between panels (runtime handles)
- Focus indicator (optional marker)

## Tests Required
- [ ] StatusLine left/center/right positioning
- [ ] StatusLine separator rendering
- [ ] StatusLine truncation behavior
- [ ] Panel border rendering
- [ ] Panel title positioning
- [ ] Panel focus style change
- [ ] Panel with various content types
- [ ] Empty status line

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] StatusLine with 3 regions
- [ ] StatusItem variants
- [ ] Panel wrapper widget
- [ ] Focus styling
- [ ] Integration with layouts
